jokes.text: The last test he gave, I got a four, and I cheated.
jokes.setup: Will explains that Professor Burton became a harder grader after his divorce
jokes.punchline: The reveal that Will got a 4 even while cheating shows just how impossible Burton became
jokes.explanation: Strong escalation joke that efficiently establishes both the character situation and gets a big laugh through the absurd reveal

jokes.text: I'll finish them tonight... right after I move the last of my things out of our... Her garage.
jokes.setup: Burton starts to say 'our garage' referring to his marital home
jokes.punchline: The self-correction to 'her garage' reveals his bitter divorce situation
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment that builds sympathy while getting a laugh from the awkward correction

jokes.text: I guess I won't be needing that anymore.
jokes.setup: Burton is talking about professional behavior at the university
jokes.punchline: He throws away his wedding ring, showing how his divorce is affecting his professional life
jokes.explanation: Visual gag that deepens character while providing dark humor about divorce

jokes.text: Behold. My chicktionary.
jokes.setup: Will says he has something Burton needs to know on a need-to-know basis
jokes.punchline: The portmanteau 'chicktionary' for his little black book
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's very quotable and perfectly fits Will's character - the kind of thing that becomes a catchphrase

jokes.text: There's a lot of F's. That ain't necessarily a bad thing, you know?
jokes.setup: Burton notices lots of F grades next to girls' names in Will's book
jokes.punchline: Will's double entendre about F's being good in a sexual context
jokes.explanation: Solid double entendre that works for the character but isn't particularly original

jokes.text: What's the B mean? Be prepared for a possible F.
jokes.setup: Burton asks about the B grade in Will's dating book
jokes.punchline: Will's explanation that B means 'be prepared for a possible F' continues the grading system joke
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation of the grading system concept with logical internal consistency

jokes.text: Oh, and G.G.W.A.B. Means Good God What A Booty.
jokes.setup: Will continues explaining his chicktionary system
jokes.punchline: The elaborate acronym for commenting on someone's posterior
jokes.explanation: Callback to the chicktionary with Will's characteristic irreverent humor

jokes.text: Well, is she involved with anyone? Mostly just herself.
jokes.setup: Burton asks if Hilary is involved with anyone
jokes.punchline: Will's joke about Hilary being self-absorbed
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy that efficiently establishes Hilary's narcissism while delivering a solid punchline

jokes.text: Maybe she could do the same for my grade.
jokes.setup: Burton says a woman like Hilary could lift his spirits
jokes.punchline: He reveals he's hoping this will influence his grading of Will
jokes.explanation: Nice dark humor showing Burton's willingness to compromise his ethics

jokes.text: You mean your knife and fork?
jokes.setup: Philip asks Geoffrey to fetch his tools for assembling the crib
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's sarcastic reference to Philip's lack of handyman skills
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey snark that plays to his character as the sardonic butler

jokes.text: Where do they get these stories?
jokes.setup: Hilary describes a romance novel about a woman mourning her dead lover
jokes.punchline: She doesn't realize she's describing her own situation with Trevor
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic irony that works on multiple levels and shows good character work

jokes.text: But I think if Trevor were still alive, he'd want me to see other guys.
jokes.setup: Hilary talks about feeling guilty about wanting to date
jokes.punchline: The matter-of-fact way she mentions Trevor being dead
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with Hilary's characteristic obliviousness

jokes.text: Boy, don't make me have to turn the hose on y'all two.
jokes.setup: Burton is reciting romantic poetry to Hilary
jokes.punchline: Will's interruption treating them like dogs in heat
jokes.explanation: Will's street-smart reaction to the overly romantic scene

jokes.text: I'm saying, he's an English professor and... And you speak some English.
jokes.setup: Will tries to explain why Hilary and Burton have something in common
jokes.punchline: The backhanded compliment about Hilary speaking 'some' English
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will-style insult disguised as matchmaking, great character moment

jokes.text: Shields are down, Scotty. Fire!
jokes.setup: Hilary responds positively to Burton's awkward romantic approach
jokes.punchline: Will's Star Trek reference treating the romance like a battle
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that works for Will's character but isn't particularly original

jokes.text: But don't you think Italy is more of a second date kind of thing.
jokes.setup: Burton mentions tickets to a gallery opening in Venice
jokes.punchline: Hilary assumes he means Venice, Italy rather than Venice Beach
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary misunderstanding that shows her privileged worldview

jokes.text: Well, duh.
jokes.setup: Burton quotes poetry about not needing ornaments for true beauty
jokes.punchline: Hilary's dismissive response shows she missed the compliment entirely
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character moment showing Hilary's obliviousness

jokes.text: I need a price check on a Peacock athletic supporter. Size small.
jokes.setup: Will is apparently shopping and needs a price check
jokes.punchline: The embarrassing announcement of needing a small jockstrap
jokes.explanation: Low-level embarrassment humor that doesn't rise above cheap physical comedy

jokes.text: Never mind.
jokes.setup: Will asked for a price check on a small athletic supporter
jokes.punchline: He quickly backs out of the embarrassing situation
jokes.explanation: Weak follow-up to an already weak joke setup

jokes.text: I'll make the test one question, open book.
jokes.setup: Will points out that Burton will have to spend all weekend grading a big midterm
jokes.punchline: Burton immediately offers to make it ridiculously easy
jokes.explanation: Shows how quickly Burton is willing to compromise his standards

jokes.text: Welcome to the family, man.
jokes.setup: Burton makes Will's test extremely easy
jokes.punchline: Will treats Burton like family now that he's being helpful
jokes.explanation: Functional character moment showing Will's transactional approach to relationships

jokes.text: Rhetta, B.
jokes.setup: Will hangs up the phone after talking to a girl named Rhetta
jokes.punchline: He writes 'B' in his chicktionary, continuing the grading system
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the chicktionary concept, good visual gag

jokes.text: He has a mole.
jokes.setup: Hilary says she's never going to see Burton again despite having fun
jokes.punchline: She reveals the superficial reason is a mole
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment showing her superficial nature with efficient delivery

jokes.text: I sit in class with this man all day, every day, and I ain't ever seen no mole.
jokes.setup: Hilary complains about Burton's mole
jokes.punchline: Will's defensive reaction as if the mole doesn't exist
jokes.explanation: Good character reaction showing Will's investment in this working out

jokes.text: But as the night went on, it got bigger and bigger... until it was all I could see.
jokes.setup: Hilary explains she hardly noticed the mole at first
jokes.punchline: Her description of becoming obsessed with it during the date
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that captures the psychology of fixating on a flaw

jokes.text: Oh, stop.
jokes.setup: Will tells Hilary she's not perfect either
jokes.punchline: Her dismissive reaction as if the idea is ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Pure Hilary narcissism delivered efficiently

jokes.text: Like in Philly, a big butt on a sister was considered a thing of beauty. You know, the more back baby got, the better. I mean, up to a point. Then, it's just nasty.
jokes.setup: Will tries to explain how imperfections can be beautiful
jokes.punchline: His detailed explanation about preferences escalating to 'then it's just nasty'
jokes.explanation: Good Will monologue with nice escalation and character voice

jokes.text: Oh, the mole man. Tell him I moved.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey announces Burton is on the phone
jokes.punchline: Hilary's immediate nickname for him and avoidance tactic
jokes.explanation: Shows Hilary's shallow nature and avoidance of confrontation

jokes.text: Hello, Spot... I mean, Scott.
jokes.setup: Hilary reluctantly takes Burton's call
jokes.punchline: She accidentally calls him 'Spot' because of his mole fixation
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment that shows her fixation has gotten worse

jokes.text: I'll work mole patrol.
jokes.setup: Will suggests doubling to take Hilary's mind off Burton's mole
jokes.punchline: His offer to work 'mole patrol' during the date
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's quotable and shows Will trying to help

jokes.text: Game, set, match. Now, stop whining. What's next?
jokes.setup: Carlton complains about the crib assembly taking forever
jokes.punchline: Philip's tennis metaphor followed by dismissing Carlton's concerns
jokes.explanation: Shows Philip's stubbornness and competitive nature

jokes.text: Yeah, they gypped us, though. They only gave us the tails.
jokes.setup: Burton compliments the lobster dinner
jokes.punchline: Will's complaint that they only got tails, not understanding that's the premium part
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Will's unfamiliarity with fine dining

jokes.text: You are making a mountain out of a mole, Hil.
jokes.setup: Hilary is clearly distracted during the opera date
jokes.punchline: Will's play on 'making a mountain out of a molehill' referencing Burton's mole
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that perfectly fits the situation and is highly quotable

jokes.text: Did you see how huge his Adam's apple is?
jokes.setup: Hilary says she's over the mole
jokes.punchline: She immediately finds a new physical flaw to obsess over
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Hilary's pattern of finding flaws

jokes.text: I mean, what's in there, a basketball?
jokes.setup: Hilary describes Burton's Adam's apple bouncing up and down
jokes.punchline: Her exaggerated question about what could be inside
jokes.explanation: Good visual absurdity that escalates Hilary's obsession

jokes.text: You're telling me that Trevor was reincarnated in Scott's throat?
jokes.setup: Will says what she's seeing is Trevor
jokes.punchline: Hilary's literal interpretation of Will's metaphor
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment showing Hilary's literal-mindedness

jokes.text: You'll become one of them crazy old women... who walk around all day long with a shower cap on... with an old raggedy halter-top that say 'Jam.'
jokes.setup: Will warns Hilary about what will happen if she doesn't date again
jokes.punchline: His specific vision of her as a crazy old woman with a 'Jam' halter-top
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific absurd imagery that's both funny and oddly believable

jokes.text: And then you gonna start wearing rhinestone gauchos.
jokes.setup: Will continues his vision of Hilary's future as a crazy old woman
jokes.punchline: The specific addition of rhinestone gauchos to the outfit
jokes.explanation: Great continuation of the absurd imagery with specific fashion details

jokes.text: [Incomprehensible rambling sound]
jokes.setup: Will saying Hilary will ramble for no reason
jokes.punchline: Actually demonstrates the crazy rambling
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy element - Will Smith's performance adds to the absurd prophecy

jokes.text: The only man you're gonna be able to get is some fool named Grady... who be smelling like menthol all the time and falling asleep in his soup.
jokes.setup: Will describes the worst part of Hilary's potential future
jokes.punchline: The specific character of Grady with his menthol smell and soup-sleeping
jokes.explanation: Masterful escalation with incredibly specific, vivid character details that became iconic

jokes.text: Oh, God, I hate soup.
jokes.setup: Will's elaborate description of her terrible future with Grady
jokes.punchline: Hilary focuses on the least important detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary reaction showing her priorities and missing the point

jokes.text: Then you march through those curtains and you look his throat in the face!
jokes.setup: Hilary is motivated by Will's description of her terrible future
jokes.punchline: Will's rallying cry to 'look his throat in the face'
jokes.explanation: Great absurd imagery that pays off the Adam's apple fixation

jokes.text: Hey did we miss the national anthem?
jokes.setup: Will and Hilary return from their dramatic conversation
jokes.punchline: Will's casual question as if nothing happened
jokes.explanation: Nice deflection showing Will's ability to play it cool

jokes.text: Some fool named Grady.
jokes.setup: Will makes a throwaway comment to himself
jokes.punchline: Callback to his earlier description of Hilary's potential future boyfriend
jokes.explanation: Nice quiet callback that rewards attentive viewers

jokes.text: It's like watching a snake digest a rabbit.
jokes.setup: Hilary complains that Burton's neck is blocking her view
jokes.punchline: Her vivid comparison to a snake digesting prey
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual metaphor that's both grotesque and hilarious, perfectly captures the image

jokes.text: Did you see Throw Momma From The Train?
jokes.setup: Will is frustrated with Burton's neck
jokes.punchline: His reference to the movie about murder, implying violent thoughts
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that works but isn't particularly original

jokes.text: No, not at all. A little.
jokes.setup: Hilary asks if Will was just using her to get a good grade
jokes.punchline: Will's immediate contradiction of his denial
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic honesty with great economy and timing

jokes.text: Hey, look, Pavarotti, go in there... and tell my date I'll be back in a minute.
jokes.setup: Will needs to chase after Hilary
jokes.punchline: He calls Burton 'Pavarotti' because they're at the opera
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Will's quick thinking under pressure

jokes.text: Mom, do me a favor. Don't have anymore babies.
jokes.setup: Carlton has just finished assembling the dangerous crib with Philip
jokes.punchline: His exhausted request to stop having children
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carlton's trauma from the experience

jokes.text: According to this, the crib malfunctioned... and an infant was tossed 50 feet in the air.
jokes.setup: Carlton reads a Consumer magazine report about the crib
jokes.punchline: The absurd danger of babies being launched into the air
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about baby safety that's absurd enough to be funny rather than disturbing

jokes.text: We can beat that.
jokes.setup: Carlton reports that a baby was tossed 50 feet by the crib
jokes.punchline: Philip's competitive response treating it like a challenge
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor showing Philip's competitive nature taken to an absurd extreme

jokes.text: Even if it is a deathtrap.
jokes.setup: Vivian tries to be supportive of Philip and Carlton's bonding
jokes.punchline: Her casual acknowledgment that they built something dangerous
jokes.explanation: Nice deadpan delivery that undercuts the sentimental moment

jokes.text: Look: It's a bird. It's a plane. It's Master Nicky.
jokes.setup: The crib malfunctions and launches baby Nicky
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's Superman reference as the baby flies through the air
jokes.explanation: Good visual payoff to the setup about dangerous cribs, with classic Geoffrey commentary

jokes.text: When I was a little girl, I never had any imaginary friends. I just used to play with Carlton's.
jokes.setup: Hilary says she never had much of an imagination
jokes.punchline: She reveals she used Carlton's imaginary friends instead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant joke that reveals character dynamics and is perfectly constructed

jokes.text: And, God, they were such dweebs.
jokes.setup: Hilary admits to using Carlton's imaginary friends
jokes.punchline: Her disdainful assessment of Carlton's imaginary friends
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that makes the previous joke even funnier while staying in character

jokes.text: I gotta go study for the massacre at little big mole.
jokes.setup: Will needs to leave to study for his midterm
jokes.punchline: His play on 'Little Bighorn' referencing Burton's mole
jokes.explanation: Clever historical reference pun that ties back to the mole storyline

jokes.text: Shortly after, her parents demanded he be removed from the college.
jokes.setup: The epilogue states Burton began dating Rhetta
jokes.punchline: The implication that his dating led to serious consequences
jokes.explanation: Dark humor epilogue that suggests Burton's relationships continued to be problematic

jokes.text: She remembers nothing.
jokes.setup: The epilogue states Hilary is currently dating
jokes.punchline: The matter-of-fact statement about her forgetting everything
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary character summary that's both funny and true to her nature

jokes.text: Baby Nicky only made it to 42 feet.
jokes.setup: The epilogue continues with updates on characters
jokes.punchline: The disappointing distance Nicky was launched compared to other babies
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that pays off the earlier setup about the dangerous crib distance
